perturbation.original_text: The Party receiving Confidential Information (“Recipient”) shall make use of the Confidential Information only for the Purpose and, except as provided below, shall not provide the Confidential Information to any other person without the express written consent of the party disclosing the Confidential Information ( “Disclosing Party ”).
perturbation.changed_text: The Party receiving Confidential Information (“Recipient”) shall make use of the Confidential Information only for the Project and, except as provided below, shall not provide the Confidential Information to any other person without the express written consent of the company disclosing the Confidential Information ( “Disclosing Company ”).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Purpose' to 'Project' and 'Disclosing Party' to 'Disclosing Company' introduces ambiguity. The original text is very clear in defining who the 'Disclosing Party' is - one of the Parties entering into the agreement, while 'Purpose' refers to acquisition of Anaheim's interest in SONGS. Now the contract must be reviewed to determine if 'Project' and 'Disclosing Company' are meant to represent the same entitites.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient will notify each emp loyee, agent, affiliate , representative, or consultant involved in the business discussions or who may have any occasion to view, handle, or obtain any of the Confidential Information, of the terms of this Agreement and shall require such person to enter i nto an agreement with terms similar to this Agreement . The Parties further agree that such Confidential Information will only be provided by Recipient to those of its directors, officers, elected or appointed officials , employees, agents, affiliates , advis ors, representatives, or consultants with a need to know.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Purpose' to 'Project' and 'Disclosing Party' to 'Disclosing Company' introduces ambiguity, especially with regards to the contradicted section. The original text clearly defines the 'Disclosing Party' as a party entering the agreement, while the change leaves it open to interpretation as to whether 'Disclosing Company' refers to the same entity. The term 'Purpose' which meant the acquisition of SONGS has also been changed to Project which creates ambiguity. This misalignment of terminology creates a strong in-text contradiction as it introduces uncertainty within the agreement regarding the identification of parties and the scope of information use, particularly regarding who is obligated to maintain confidentiality as outlined in the contradicted section, which focuses on the recipient's responsibilities to their employees and affiliates.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will disclose only such information as it determines is legally required and will use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain confidential treatme nt for any Confidential Information that is so disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will disclose only such information as it determines is legally required and will use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain private treatme nt for any Confidential Information that is so disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'confidential treatment' to 'private treatment' can cause confusion. 'Confidential treatment' has a legal meaning, especially in the context of disclosures required by law. 'Private treatment' is more vague and could be interpreted differently, weakening the protection for the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient is not required to seek or obtain a judicial determination establishing confidential treatment, but it will cooperated in any efforts to do so by Disclosing Party. The address and phone number for notification to Anaheim is: City of Anaheim, Attention: Public Utilities General Manager, 201 South Anaheim Blvd., Suite 1101, Anaheim, California 92805 and (714) 765 - 5173. The address and phone number for notification of Respondent is: [Insert Respondent’s Contact Name, Phone, Address, and email] .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'confidential treatment' to 'private treatment' weakens the legal protection for the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information. The contradicted text refers to 'establishing confidential treatment'. The changed text introduces a vague term 'private treatment', making it unclear whether efforts to obtain 'private treatment' satisfy the requirement to establish 'confidential treatment'. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency within the document, indicating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and Recipient’s duty to protect Confidential Information expires the longer of three (3) years from the date set forth above or after the conclusion of the Decommissioning of SONGS is completed , unless the Parties agree in writing to a different term .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and Recipient’s duty to protect Confidential Materials expires the longer of three (3) years from the date set forth above or after the conclusion of the Decommissioning of SONGS is completed , unless the Individuals agree in writing to a different term .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Materials' and 'Parties' to 'Individuals' creates ambiguity. Previously, 'Confidential Information' had a specific definition, but now the agreement requires determining whether 'Confidential Materials' is an interchangeable term or if it includes a wider, or narrower, scope of information. Changing 'Parties' to 'Individuals' in just this sentence adds to the confusion previously introduced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Standard of Care . Recipient shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized use, disclosure, dissemination, or publication of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Materials' and 'Parties' to 'Individuals' introduces ambiguity and contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the contradicted text. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Confidential Information,' but the changed text creates uncertainty about whether 'Confidential Materials' is the same, wider, or narrower, therefore creating ambiguity about the standard of care required. Similarly, changing Parties to Individuals adds more confusion because 'Parties' would've been a defined term and now an individual would be tasked with the responsibility that a Party in the agreement would've had, which leads to a direct conflict and misalignment of previously agreed terms, which is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The s upplying of Confidential Information shall not be considered to provide any license or proprietary rights, including any implied patent license or intellectual property rights in the property of the other by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The s upplying of Secret Information shall not be considered to provide any license or proprietary rights, including any implied patent license or intellectual property rights in the property of the other by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Secret Information' introduces inconsistency. The entire agreement revolves around the definition and protection of 'Confidential Information,' and now, 'Secret Information' is used without defining it. This may lead to confusion about whether 'Secret Information' falls under the protections outlined for 'Confidential Information,' or if it's a different category altogether.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the Parties desire that all Purpose information provided by Anaheim (whether created by Anaheim or created for Anaheim’s use by a third party and whether or not specifically described in the prior paragraph) be treated by Respondent as Confidential Information under this Agreement. WHEREAS, the P arties desire that there be no oral or written disclosure to third par ties of the nature and extent of such discussions and information exchange regarding the Purpose , including no oral or written disclosure s to third parties of any such Confidential I nformation ; however, to the exten t disclosure is necessitated by law or ot herwise, it shall be made on the terms and conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Secret Information' introduces a term not previously defined in the contract. The contradicted text explicitly defines certain information as 'Confidential Information' and outlines its treatment. The introduction of 'Secret Information' creates uncertainty as to whether this new term is covered by the existing confidentiality provisions, thus creating a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding which terms should be enforced, fitting the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition. This contradiction is strong because it undermines the entire confidentiality framework of the agreement.
